original_thesis: The full 'why people do what they do' stack. Incentives create the field; game theory predicts the plays; signaling reveals hidden information; social proof amplifies; commitment locks in; principal-agent misaligns.
corrected_thesis: The 'why people do what they do' stack. Incentives create the motivational field; game-theory-payoffs predicts strategic choices; signaling reveals hidden information through costly actions; social-proof amplifies behavior through imitation; principal-agent-problem explains misalignment between delegator and executor; goal-setting channels effort toward declared targets. This stack maps the structural forces that drive human behavior in organizations and markets.
what_this_stack_defeats: Naive trust, surface-level motivation analysis, ignoring structural incentives
notes: Original thesis mentioned commitment-and-consistency-bias — not a member. Goal-setting replaces it in the detected cluster, which shifts the stack slightly from 'bias awareness' toward 'motivation architecture'. Both framings are coherent.
